title: Question: Consumer doesn't match lhs of assign
body:
---


Author Name: **Dennis Muhlestein**
Original Redmine Message: 1402 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Today I updated from a fairly old build to the latest git version of verilator.  
I got this error on a simple register array declaration:

  reg [31:0] wfifo [0:63];

Error:

Consumer doesn't match lhs of assign

I traced the problem down to the a commit (3dd552..) that turned on a "duplicate clock gate" optimization.  Anyway, sure enough, if I use option -Od the error doesn't occur as that is probably the behavior I was using all along.  Question is... I'm having a hard time finding what is wrong with the source if I wanted to leave the command line alone and get the default behavior.  The error isn't making much sense to me and I'm not sure where there may or may not be a problem.  For what it's worth the module is just used for sim although we've been using it in a test bench for quite some time.
